summary:
The patient should have her serum estradiol level checked alongside further evaluation of potential causes such as genetic factors or autoimmune disorders given that both high FSH and low estrogen support a diagnosis of primary ovarian insufficiency. This approach helps confirm the condition while ruling out other possible etiologies affecting reproductive health. To summarize:

The next steps include measuring serum estradiol along with evaluating potential underlying causes; confirmed by elevated FSH and decreased estradiol indicating likely primary ovarian insufficiency. Ruling out alternative diagnoses through comprehensive assessment ensures accurate management planning. ```plaintext
To diagnose primary ovarian insufficiency accurately, measure serum estradiol levels since elevated FSH combined with lowered estradiol confirms the condition. Comprehensive evaluation including assessing genetic and immune-related factors will help rule out alternate explanations and guide appropriate treatment.
```
